title: WASM Feature Surface Slimming
problem: Merman's current core and browser-WASM dependency surfaces are too broad for non-JS WebAssembly hosts such as Typst plugins.
gates: cargo run -p xtask -- profile-budget check-deps --profile pure-wasm --package merman-core --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --depth 3
cargo run -p xtask -- profile-budget check-wasm --profile typst-wasm --wasm <typst-plugin.wasm>
cargo nextest run -p merman-core -p merman-render -p merman-bindings-core

evidence.note: Typst plugins use wasmi and the wasm-minimal-protocol import surface, not wasm-bindgen browser glue.
Parser-only local probe produced a 4.3 MB wasm with 12 wasm-bindgen/js-sys imports, including WebCrypto and JS Date.
Current browser wasm-size default produced an 8.5 MB wasm with 49 wasm-bindgen/js imports; no-default produced 2.7 MB with 25 imports.

WFS-060 split full YAML frontmatter and JSON5 directives into full-config; merman-core --no-default-features no longer links serde_yaml, unsafe-libyaml, json5, or pest while preserving common inline metadata parsing.
WFS-070 split DOMPurify-like HTML rewriting and URL canonicalization into full-sanitization; no-default core no longer links lol_html, url, ICU/idna URL dependencies, or browser/host crates.

Typst bridge-only stripped wasm is 33,412 bytes; default render stripped wasm is 5,414,863 bytes; core-full is 6,262,880 bytes; ratex-math is 8,238,005 bytes. Default render size is now dominated by real code/data, not custom metadata.

WFS-080 made detector, semantic parser, typed render parser, and supported metadata projections profile-aware from BaselineRegistryProfile; tiny/no-default excludes mindmap, architecture, and flowchart-elk parser/render registrations.

WFS-090 started with `xtask wasm-size-matrix`, which builds browser and Typst feature presets separately and reports raw, stripped, gzip, and brotli wasm bytes. CI budgets are recorded in docs/release/WASM_SIZE_BUDGETS.json, and full matrices are recorded in PACKAGE_SURFACES.md and EVIDENCE_AND_GATES.md without conflating wasm-bindgen browser packages with Typst/pure-wasm packages.

WFS-090 finished by adding preset-aware browser package builds (`browser-core`, `browser-render`, `browser-ascii`, `browser-full`, `browser-ratex-math`), a `bindingCapabilities()` export, and capability-aware smoke/prepack checks. Default npm publication remains `browser-full`; ASCII retains the full core registry because it depends on the full browser ASCII implementation.

WFS-100 validated the existing wasm-minimal-protocol Typst plugin transport. The default render artifact imports only the two `typst_env` protocol functions, exports `memory` plus plugin functions, and passes `xtask typst-plugin-smoke` through a wasmi host call returning SVG JSON.

WFS-110 recorded release semantics: `@mermanjs/web` remains one npm package publishing `browser-full`; browser slim artifacts remain source-build presets; `merman-wasm` is browser/wasm-bindgen only; `merman-typst-plugin` owns Typst-compatible wasm-minimal-protocol builds and gates.

WFS-120 closed the lane after final verification: rust fmt, core/render/bindings nextest, pure/typst dependency gates, Typst import/smoke gates, WASM size budget gate, and all browser preset build/smoke checks passed. A follow-up web packaging cut moved wasm-pack to the workspace wasm-size profile and reduced generated browser-full wasm from 8,648,002 to 5,580,151 raw bytes. Residual work is split into future slim npm publication, Typst registry packaging, and deeper render/data slimming.

continue_policy.notes: Lane closed after WFS-010 through WFS-120. Continue in separate follow-on lanes for public slim browser package contracts, Typst registry packaging, or deeper render/data size reductions.
